name: Nagaland
landmark: Hornbill Festival at Kisama Heritage Village
stopoverNote: Dimapur airport/rail is the practical gateway, with road access onward to Kohima in about 3–4 hours and very long hill-road drives to Mokokchung and Mon. Indian visitors need a Nagaland Inner Line Permit before entry; the official portal lists online tourist ILP at ₹200/person valid 30 days, but verify the fee before travel. Hornbill Festival runs 1–10 December at Kisama near Kohima and sharply raises room/vehicle prices, so book Kohima/Kisama stays and taxis well ahead.

diet.vegetarian: Nagaland is meat-, pork-, smoked-meat, bamboo-shoot and axone-heavy, so vegetarian travel is easiest in Kohima and Dimapur cafés, hotels and Baptist/market-area eateries. Ask for rice, dal, boiled vegetables, aloo curry, veg thukpa, steamed veg momos and plain chow; in Khonoma, Mokokchung and Mon, pre-request vegetarian homestay meals before arrival.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is possible but requires advance notice. Remote village homestays often cook one fixed meal for everyone and may use fermented fish, smoked meat stock, egg, milk tea or butter; ask for rice, dal, boiled greens and vegetables cooked only in oil. Axone is soybean-based but is often cooked with pork, and bamboo-shoot dishes may contain meat stock, so confirm clearly.
phrases: Moi maas aru pork nakhai = I do not eat meat or pork
Shakahari khana, dim aru dudh bina dibo = please give vegetarian food without egg or milk
Oil te pakao, butter/ghee nidibo = cook in oil, do not add butter or ghee

cities.name: Dimapur
cities.notes: Nagaland's airport and rail gateway, useful for ILP checks, cash, Kachari ruins and a low-stress first or last night before hill-road travel.

cities.name: Kohima
cities.notes: The core Nagaland base for World War II history, State Museum, Kisama Heritage Village, Hornbill Festival and the Dzukou Valley trek approach.

cities.name: Khonoma
cities.notes: Angami green village near Kohima where homestays, terraced fields, conservation stories and village walks work best with a local guide.

cities.name: Mokokchung
cities.notes: Ao Naga cultural base with Ungma, Longkhum, Chuchuyimlang, churches, shawls and a gentler alternative to the far Mon road.

cities.name: Mon
cities.notes: Remote Konyak country for Longwa, Shangnyu and tattooed-elder heritage; rewarding but logistically demanding on rough, slow hill roads.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel work in Dimapur and Kohima, with patchier service on the Dzukou trail and long Mon/Mokokchung hill roads; BSNL can help in some remote pockets. Download offline maps, keep driver and homestay numbers saved, and carry cash because card/UPI reliability drops outside larger towns.

Dimapur.name: Dimapur
Dimapur.note: Use Dimapur as a functional gateway day: finish ILP checks, withdraw cash, rest after flights/trains and avoid starting mountain roads late.

days.theme: Gateway arrival, ILP check & Kachari ruins
days.note: Do not treat Dimapur as a throwaway transfer if you land late. Confirm your Nagaland ILP before leaving the gateway and keep a printed/offline copy for checkposts and hotels.

activities.name: Arrive at Dimapur airport or railway station
activities.tip: Dimapur is the best place to withdraw cash and buy SIM/data top-ups before hill districts.

activities.name: Nagaland ILP document check
activities.cost: ₹200/person tourist ILP listed by official portal; verify before travel
activities.tip: Official guidance says online tourist ILP may be approved within hours during working time but can take 2–3 working days; apply before arrival.

activities.name: Kachari Ruins
activities.tip: Go in daylight; the ruins are most useful as a history stop before the Kohima drive.

Kohima.name: Kohima
Kohima.note: Four days gives an arrival/city day, one history-and-museum day, one Kisama/Hornbill day and one honest full Dzukou trek day; add a fifth for weather or festival crowds.

days.theme: Dimapur to Kohima and gentle capital orientation
days.note: Dimapur to Kohima is a winding 3–4 hour hill-road transfer in good conditions and slower in rain or festival traffic. Keep the day light and avoid Dzukou or Kisama on arrival afternoon.

activities.name: Dimapur to Kohima road transfer
activities.tip: Start early in December because Hornbill traffic and hotel check-ins can add delays.

activities.name: Kohima local market and supply stop
activities.tip: Buy water, snacks and rain layers before Dzukou; veg snacks are easier here than on remote roads.

activities.name: Evening at a Kohima café or viewpoint

meals: Rice with dal and local greens

days.theme: World War II history, State Museum & Kohima culture
days.note: This day stays within Kohima and is intentionally clusterable. Visit solemn memorial spaces respectfully and ask before photographing people or church services.

activities.name: Kohima War Cemetery
activities.tip: Morning light is best; keep voices low at the Commonwealth memorial.

activities.name: Nagaland State Museum
activities.tip: Use this before village visits so textiles, morungs, ornaments and clan symbols make more sense.

activities.name: Kohima handloom and handicraft browsing
activities.tip: Buy directly from cooperatives or clearly priced shops where possible.

days.theme: Kisama Heritage Village and Hornbill Festival focus
days.note: Kisama is about 12 km from Kohima. During Hornbill Festival, 1–10 December, traffic, entry queues, room rates and taxi prices jump; outside festival dates the heritage village is quieter and may have fewer live performances.

activities.name: Kisama Heritage Village morung walk
activities.tip: Ask before entering morungs or photographing performers, elders and ceremonial objects.

activities.name: Hornbill Festival arena and tribal performances
activities.tip: The festival runs annually 1–10 December and is the biggest trip driver for Nagaland. Book stays and vehicles months ahead.

activities.name: Kisama craft stalls and food courts
activities.tip: Vegetarians should ask about pork stock, dry fish and axone before ordering.

activities.name: Return to Kohima before late-night traffic

meals: Rice and vegetable curry at festival stalls
Smoked bamboo-shoot dishes only if meat-free

days.note: Treat Dzukou as its own strenuous full day, not an add-on. Incredible India describes the trek as roughly 9 km and 5–6 hours with steep, slippery sections; start before dawn, hire a local guide, and skip in heavy rain or if knees/fitness are poor.

activities.name: Early drive to Viswema or Jakhama trailhead
activities.tip: Viswema is longer by road but generally clearer underfoot; Jakhama is steeper and can be slippery.

activities.name: Dzukou Valley trek with local guide
activities.cost: Guide/entry/camp fees vary; budget ₹1,500–₹3,000 extra and verify locally
activities.tip: Carry rain protection, packed food, warm layer and headlamp; network can disappear on the trail.

Khonoma.name: Khonoma
Khonoma.note: Khonoma is close to Kohima but deserves a slow guided day or overnight because village etiquette, conservation history and terraces are the point.

days.theme: Khonoma green village, terraces & Angami stories
days.note: Hire a village guide and keep the route gentle after the Dzukou day. Ask before photographing homes, granaries, elders or weaving work, and dress modestly in village lanes.

activities.name: Kohima to Khonoma short hill drive
Guided Khonoma village and fort walk
activities.cost: Guide fee varies; budget ₹800–₹1,500 and verify locally
activities.tip: A guide helps explain Angami conservation rules, clan spaces and the village's resistance history.

activities.name: Terraced-field viewpoint and conservation area edge
Homestay meal or tea with advance dietary request
activities.tip: Vegetarian/vegan travellers should request meat-free stock and oil-only cooking when booking.

meals: Dal and vegetable curry by request

Mokokchung.name: Mokokchung
Mokokchung.note: Two days is the minimum: one honest transfer day from Kohima/Khonoma and one Ao-village culture day. Add a third for festival dates, Sunday church or slower village stays.

days.theme: Long road transfer to Ao country
days.note: Kohima/Khonoma to Mokokchung is a long hill-road day and can run 6–8 hours with roadwork, fog or rain. Keep sightseeing light and arrive before dark.

activities.name: Khonoma/Kohima to Mokokchung drive
activities.tip: Carry snacks, water and cash; do not plan a same-day Mon connection.

activities.name: Town market for Ao shawls and supplies

days.note: Focus on nearby Ao cultural sites rather than trying to cover distant districts. Sunday mornings are church-focused; be discreet and ask before entering services.

activities.name: Ungma village heritage walk
activities.tip: Ungma is one of the oldest Ao settlements; local interpretation makes the visit meaningful.

activities.name: Longkhum village viewpoint and folklore stop
activities.cost: Taxi/guide varies; budget ₹2,000–₹3,500 for a local loop
activities.tip: Roads are narrow; return before fog or dusk.

activities.name: Ao cultural museum or local craft stop
Chuchuyimlang village extension
activities.tip: Add only if roads are dry and you are not driving onward the same day.

Mon.name: Mon
Mon.note: Mon is remote enough to justify three days: a long transfer, one Longwa/Konyak heritage day and one buffer or onward-exit day. Do not cram it into a Kohima weekend.

days.theme: Mokokchung to Mon remote hill-road transfer
days.note: This is one of the itinerary's hardest logistics days: roads are rough, distances are slow, and after-dark driving is a bad idea. Start early and keep only a light Mon-town orientation on arrival.

activities.name: Mokokchung to Mon drive
activities.tip: Ask your driver about current road conditions; monsoon damage can change timings dramatically.

activities.name: Mon town check-in and permit copy check
Mon bazaar orientation
activities.tip: Use the market for snacks and water before village drives.

meals: Rice with dal and greens by request

days.theme: Longwa and Konyak heritage with etiquette
days.note: Longwa is close to the India–Myanmar border and needs a respectful local guide. Headhunting history is sensitive living heritage, not a costume show; ask before photos and avoid intrusive questions to elders.

activities.name: Longwa village guided walk
activities.tip: Do not photograph tattooed elders, homes or ceremonial objects without explicit permission.

meals: Boiled greens and dal if available

days.theme: Shangnyu, Veda Peak option or Assam exit buffer
days.note: Keep this as a flexible day because remote-road conditions decide whether Shangnyu/Veda Peak or an early Assam exit is wiser. If flying from Dibrugarh/Jorhat, leave a safety night outside Mon.

activities.name: Veda Peak viewpoint if roads are clear
activities.tip: Skip in fog, rain or if you need to exit Mon the same day.

activities.name: Mon to Assam exit planning
activities.cost: Mon→Dibrugarh/Jorhat side vehicle varies; verify locally
activities.tip: Use this as a logistics buffer rather than booking a tight same-evening flight.

connections.method: Shared taxi or private vehicle on NH-29 hill road
Local taxi via short hill road
Private vehicle via Kohima/Wokha-side hill roads
Private vehicle on slow, rough inter-district roads

extras: Nagaland ILP is mandatory for Indian visitors; apply online before travel and carry printed/offline copies with photo ID for hotels and checkposts.
The official ILP portal lists domestic tourist ILP at ₹200/person valid for 30 days and says online tourist approvals can be quick during working hours, but allow 2–3 working days as a buffer.
Hornbill Festival is held 1–10 December at Kisama and is the state's biggest demand spike: reserve Kohima/Kisama accommodation, vehicles and guides months ahead and expect premium pricing.
Dzukou Valley is a strenuous full-day trek with steep/slippery sections; use a local guide, do not combine it with Kisama or Khonoma, and skip in heavy monsoon rain.
Village etiquette matters: ask before photos, dress modestly, do not touch ceremonial objects, and treat Konyak headhunting history as sensitive living heritage.
Vegetarian and vegan meals are much easier in Dimapur/Kohima than in fixed-menu village homestays; pre-request meat-free, fish-free and dairy-free meals before remote stays.
